references.text: [Er98] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, Some of my new and almost new problems and results in combinatorial number theory. Number theory (Eger, 1996) (1998), 169-180.

remarks.text: It is trivial that $V'(x) \leq V(x)$. In \cite{Er98} Erd\H{o}s suggests the limit may be infinite. See also [416].

statement.text: Let\[V'(x)=\#\{\phi(m) : 1\leq m\leq x\}\]and\[V(x)=\#\{\phi(m) \leq x : 1\leq m\}.\]Does $\lim V(x)/V'(x)$ exist? Is it $>1$?
